task: Write a Python function split_csvish(s) splitting the string on commas into a list of fields, trimming whitespace around each field and dropping fields that are empty after trimming. An empty string returns []. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: Code meets all criteria, correctly splitting strings with commas into lists of trimmed fields
output:
```python
def split_csvish(s):
    return [field.strip() for field in s.split(",") if field.strip()]

# Edge case: Handles strings with multiple consecutive commas, treating them as a single delimiter
```
trace: I used list comprehension to split the string on commas and strip leading/trailing whitespace from each field. I also included an if-clause to drop empty fields resulting from trimming. To handle the edge case of multiple consecutive commas, I treated them as a single delimiter while splitting the string. This ensures that lists with multiple empty entries due to extra commas will be properly handled.
